rules.title: Manifest structure may hide executable behavior
rules.description: A manifest contains duplicate keys or syntax that can confuse naive parsers.
rules.rationale: Duplicate keys, YAML merges, and TOML alternate forms can cause scanners and package tools to see different effective values.
rules.remediation: Remove duplicate keys and avoid hidden lifecycle behavior behind anchors, merges, dotted keys, or inline tables.
rules.attack_narrative: A package places a benign npm script first and a later duplicate postinstall key that executes during install.
attack_examples: A duplicate package.json scripts key hides a later postinstall command.
false_positive_examples: A fixture intentionally testing duplicate key detection.
